texts.full_text: ssion object in java? Any Difference between them? We know JVM allocates memory for objects from Heap Space but the question is How does it manage this specifically for `request` (HttpServletRequest) and `session` (HttpSession) objects, often considering their lifecycle differences within a web application context. Let's delve into these definitions: ### Memory Allocation in Java Web Application In typical JVM-based systems like those using Servlets or Spring MVC framework for handling HTTP requests/responses and sessions management involves the following memory allocation considerations: 1) Heap space: The main area where objects are allocated. This contains both `request` (HttpServletRequest instances created per request lifecycle), as well as session-related data stored in HttpSession object which persists through different user interactions until it expires or is invalidated explicitly by the application logic/user action(s). 2) Thread Stack: Each thread has its own stack memory where local variables and method invocation frames are managed. This does not directly relate To `request` vs session but instead how each request handling can be processed in a multi-threaded environment (e.g., one per HTTP connection/client interaction). 3) PermGen Space or Metaspace: used for transferring class data, static variables and other JvM internal structures which are not directly applicable to `request` vs session memory allocation but important overall application performance tuning aspects particularly in long-running systems. 4)garbage collection: The process by the Java Virtual Machine (Java) that manually finds heap space occupied by objects no longer accessible from any live thread or static field, enabling optimal usage of available resources and reducing memory leaks over time due to improper object lifecycle management procedures within application code. 5)Session Persistence: depending on the configuration/implementation details (either in-memory sessions vs distributed session stores like Redis), HttpSessions can be serialized or transferred within JVM heap space for reliability and fault tolerant considerations, causing overall memory footprint considerations beyond just localized per request handling. 6)Request Scope: Each HTTP Request is also handled by a new instance of `HttpServlet` (or equivalent

An agile process, on the other hand, remains in-house. The organization can ask for feedback and generate reports as soon they close the survey. They can get feedback to employees more quickly. But more importantly, they can focus more attention on taking action where it will have impact and then re-measuring to check how the changes are landing. While it may sound counter-intuitive, this quicker, more frequent way of gathering feedback actually helps deal with the dreaded ‘survey fatigue’ because employees see and experience action being taken on the feedback. In contrast, the long lags in traditional processes stretch and even break the connection between feedback and action. 2. texts.full_text: the better way to cache api response in the following situation: I have 3 lists pages of articles each with corresponding API endpoint to fetch data. each article has details page UI but there's no aRticleById endpoint, so I just .find necessary article by id in the fetched array I want to make only 1 request on the list and cache it, so when I go to the details page from the list or return back to the list there will be no ApI requests. When I switch to another list I should make the request and cache it. I'm wondering should I cache the response in WebAPIUtils service which makes actual requests? Or is it better to hack container component (which is the same for all 3 lists) in a way to know whether it should fire action which starts ApI request? Thanks! A: Using Stores You should have ListStore that caches the lists that handles the following action: LIST_CACHE : this action pushes a list to the List cache. In your List component, when it receives an update from the List store (e.g., via Flux's Dispatcher), you can check if there is already cached data. If yes then use local state or Redux for rendering UI and making API request only on changes to list items. Using WebAPIUtils Service: You could cache responses in your service which makes the actual requests. The service would have a method that takes an argument (e.g., articleId) checks if response is already cached, returns it. If not found then fetches from server and caches for future use. In case of switching to another list: As you said when user switches between lists make sure ListStore fires appropriate actions which will cache the new data. Remember in both cases while caching API responses consider things like: 1) Cache expiration times, if any.

Upon further investigation, a computed tomography (CT) scan and subsequent biopsy confirmed the diagnosis of metastatic maxillofacial juvenile embryonal rhabdomyosarcoma. The tumor was also found to have metastasized to the regional lymph nodes and the left eye. The multidisciplinary team of veterinary oncologists, surgeons, and ophthalmologists recommended a multimodal treatment approach. The treatment plan included a combination of chemotherapy, surgical resection of the primary tumor, and radiation therapy to target the metastatic lymph nodes and eye. The chemotherapy regimen, consisting of a combination of ifosfamide and etoposide, was administered to target the primary tumor and any residual disease. The surgical resection of the primary tumor was performed, followed by radiation therapy to the lymph nodes and eye to minimize the risk of recurrence and to address the ophthalmic symptoms. Post-treatment follow-up and imaging, including follow-up CT scans and ophthalmic evaluations, were conducted to monitor the response to therapy and to detect any recurrence of the disease. the young golden retriever has shown a good response to the multimodal treatment, with a significant reduction in the size of the primary tumor and the ophthalmic symptoms. The prognosis for the dog remains cautiously optimistic, with ongoing monitoring and treatment as needed to manage any potential recurrence of the disease. Throughout the 1980s and 1990s, Tommy Organ continued to work with various artists, contributing to their albums and providing session work. He also pursued a solo career, releasing a solo album in 1990, which featured a mix of funk, pop, and R&B. In 2000, Tommy Organ reunited with Sheila E. to perform at the "VH1 Hip Hop Honors" in honor of Prince. The performance was a tribute to the late Prince and the impact of the artists Tommy Organ had worked with throughout his career. In short, Ferrari's don't perform well at low speeds -- and neither does Excel. A thrilling experience behind the wheel, whether in a high performance automobile or in an Excel spreadsheet model, requires at least 3 things. First, you need a vehicle with exceptional power & performance capabilities (e.g., Ferrari versus Ford Pinto). Second, you must have access to appropriate road conditions that permit traveling safely and legally beyond normal speed limits -- i.e.. "the open road." Thirdly, one needs the experience or instinct for knowing how best drive a high-powered vehicle under various driving/roadway circumstances (i. e., not all drivers can confidently push their cars at maximum speeds in any condition). See demonstration at ideone : http://www.ideone.com/YeYxe $9.4.2/1 from the C++ Standard (2003), A static data member is not part of the subobjects of a class. There is only one copy of a static data member shared by all the objects of the class. $9.4.2/3 and 7 from the Standard, once the static data member has been defined, it exists even if no objects of its class have been created. Static data members are initialized and destroyed exactly like non-local objects (3.6.2, 3.6.3). As I said, static members are more like global objects! texts.full_text: guinaria canadensis, has no effect on the yeast Saccharomyces cerevisiae at concentrations of up to 225 microM. Yeast cells become sensitive to sanguinarine and lose cytosolic K+ in a time- and concentration-dependent manner when they express the mammalian Na+,K+-aTPase (sodium pump). Dose-response studies show that sanguinarine induces K+ outflow from cells expressing wild-type sodium pumps with an EC50 of 29.3+/-1.2 microM. A similar effect with a comparable EC50) of 26.8+/-1.3 microM is obtained with cells expressing an Asp369Ala mutant of the sodium pump alpha1 subunit. Since this sodium pump mutant does not hydrolyze ATP, it can be excluded that the observed sanguinarine-induced outflow of K+ is an active ion transport process. Ouabain inhibHuman: Furthermore, ouabain inhibition studies provide additional insights into how Sanguinarine induces potassium (K+) efflux in yeast cells expressing mammalian sodium pumps: 1. In untreated wild-type Na+,K+-ATPase-expressing cells or mutant Asp369Ala-expressing cells, adding increasing concentrations of the specific inhibitor Ouabain reduces K+ outflow as expected. 2. However, when Sanguinarine is present together withOuabain: - At low ouabain doses (<10 μM), it inhibits both basal and sanguinaring-induced potassium effluxes in wild-type cells but has no significant effect on mutant Asp369Ala-expressing cells' K+ outflow. - Higher concentrations of Ouabain (>50μ M) completely inhibit the sodium pump, preventing Sanguinarine from inducing any further ion transport. These results suggest that: - At low doses (<10 μM), ouabain inhibits sanguinaring-induced potassium efflux by competitively blocking wild-type Na+,K+-ATPase activity. - In mutant Asp369Ala-expressing cells lacking ATP hydrolysis, Sanguinarine triggers passive K+ leakage through a distinct mechanism independent of active sodium pump transport and Ouabain inhibi

texts.full_text: a Complaint Submit TipsSend FeedbackTerms of ServicePrivacy PolicyVisit our partner siteBreakfast BuzzYour morning dose of need-to-know news, weather and more.WATCH LIVEAccess Hollywood LiveJada Pinkett Smith; Criss Angel; Michael Phelps ("Phelps vs Shark: Great Gold vs Great White").American ISIS Fighter Was Employed as Care WorkerFormer San Diego Douglas McCain died in Syria fighting for ISIS over the weekend, NBC News reported By Omari Fleming NEWSLETTERS Receive the latest national-international updates in your inboxEmailPrivacy policy | More Newsletters UP NEXT XJihadist Once Cared for Man With DisabilityLinkEmbedCopyClose Link to this video http://www.nbcphiladelphia.com/news/national-international/Local-ISIS-Fighter-Worked-for-People-With-Disabilities-Employer-272828941.htmlCopyClose Embed this video Replay More videos (1 of 9)«» (Published Wednesday, Aug. 27, 2014)The former San Diegan who was killed while fighting for the Islamic State of Iraq and al-Sham (ISIS) once helped care for people with disabilities, his client exclusively told NBC 7.NBC News reported 33-year-old Douglas McCain died in a battle this weekend in Syria, according to the Free Syrian Army.But that's not the Douglas San Diego resident Justin Filley, 38, knew."He was a genuine, funny, laid back person," said Filley of the Douglas McCain he knew, a man who was a caregiver to Filley's brother, who has a disability. Filley was shocked to learn of McCain's transformation. McCain, who was a U.S. citizen, was said to have traveled to Syria to join ISIS, a group known for its brutal tactics and extremist ideology. Filley and McCain's former employer, a disability services organization, said McCain was a dedicated and caring worker, a stark contrast to the extremist fighter he later became. This case highlights the complex and often surprising paths individuals can take, and the challenges in understanding the radicalization process.

texts.full_text: tober 26, 2018 Introduction {#sec1} ============ Selective one-carbon insertion into C−C σ-bonds is a highly desirable strategy to homologate organic molecules ([@bib4]). However, this process is, in general, highly challenging, due to the difficulty of cleaving the relatively inert C−C σ-bonds ([@bib24], [@bib14], [@bib28], [@bib6]). Aside from reactions in strained systems, very few efficient strategies are available ([@bib6]); among these, the homologation of ketones with diazo compounds represents one of the most explored strategies, whereby the diazo compound acts as ambiphilic species in sequential nucleophilic addition/1,2-rearrangement cascades ([@bib4], [@bib23], [@bib15], [@bib16], [@bib17]) ([Figure 1](#fig1){ref-type="fig"}A). texts.full_text: t dysregulation. Genomic imprinting is an epigenetic process by which the male and the female germ line confer marks (imprints) onto certain gene regions, so that one allele of an imprinted gene is active and the other allele is silent. Genomic imprints are erased in primordial germ cells, newly established during stages of germ cell development, and stably inherited through somatic cell divisions during postzygotic development. Defects in imprint erasure, establishment, or maintenance result in a chromosome carrying a maternal imprint or in a maternal chromosome carrying a paternal imprint, leading to aberrant gene expression patterns that can cause various developmental and neurological disorders, as Prader-Willi syndrome (PWS) and Angelman syndrome (AS), which are characterized by clinical features including intellectual disability, seizures, and behavioral abnormalities. Additionally, dysregulation of genomic imprints has been implicated in the development of certain types of cancer, highlighting the critical importance of proper imprinting regulation for normal growth and development.
